oversteer_entry: Soften the front anti-roll bars or stiffen the rear slightly.
Increase front dampening compression or lower rear rebound.
Increase the initial torque on the LSD slightly to stabilize deceleration.
oversteer_exit: Lower the rear ride height relative to the front to shift weight back.
Soften the rear spring rate (Natural Frequency) to allow more mechanical grip.
Decrease the LSD acceleration sensitivity slider by 5-10 points to prevent snap.
understeer_mid_corner: Increase front negative camber by 0.2 to 0.5 degrees.
Lower the front anti-roll bar setting to let the nose bite.
Slightly soften front natural frequency or stiffen the rear.
tire_wear_management: High front wear: Reduce toe-in or lower front natural frequency to decrease scrubbing.
High rear wear: Reduce rear camber slightly or soften the rear anti-roll bar to stop sliding.
Ensure brake balance is adjusted toward the axle with lower wear during long stints.

red_bull_ring.characteristics: High traction demands, heavy braking zones, wet/slippery kerbs.
red_bull_ring.focus: Rear traction on exit of T1 and T3 is vital. Keep suspension compliant enough to ride the apex kerbs without unsettling the chassis.
adjustments: Set LSD acceleration sensitivity to a lower, progressive range (20-30) to maximize traction out of tight corners.
Soften anti-roll bars (range 3-5) to absorb sudden kerb impacts.
Add slight rear toe-in (0.15 - 0.25) to steady the rear under heavy straight-line braking.

spa_francorchamps.characteristics: High speed, fast direction changes, extreme compression loads.
spa_francorchamps.focus: High-speed stability through Eau Rouge/Raidillon and maximum aerodynamic efficiency down the Kemmel Straight.
adjustments: Stiffen Natural Frequency (Front: 3.20+, Rear: 3.40+) to prevent the floor bottoming out under compression.
Set front camber higher (3.0 - 3.5) to maintain contact patch through fast sweeping corners.
Balance downforce closer to maximum to stabilize high-speed corner transitions.
